title: Real-reader comprehension pilot (>=5 family/caregiver or advocate-proxy readers)
context: Ewing-Family-Guide is a free, plain-language guide for families, caregivers, and AYA patients facing a Ewing sarcoma diagnosis. It ONLY restates and organizes vetted-authority information (NCI/PDQ, COG, ESMO, CCLG), with a citation on every assertion, at a health-literacy reading level. This is HIGH risk-tier, patient-facing health content: nothing patient-facing ships until both a credentialed pediatric/sarcoma oncologist AND a patient/caregiver advocate sign off (version-scoped, either can block). No delivery partner, oncologist reviewer, or patient-advocate reviewer is yet secured. This task measures plain-language comprehension with real readers rather than assuming it, gathering PII-free feedback from family/caregiver or advocate-proxy readers on the dual-reviewed pages. Binding cancer guardrails apply: open-access/aggregate/de-identified educational sources only; controlled-access (dbGaP, EGA, individual-level biobanks) and any identifiable patient data are out of scope; verify each source license; provenance on every assertion; no medical advice, diagnosis, or individual prognosis.
objective: Run a real-reader comprehension pilot with >=5 family/caregiver or advocate-proxy readers and report findings to drive plain-language fixes, collecting no PII.
acceptanceCriteria: >=5 family/caregiver or advocate-proxy readers review ship-slated pages; comprehension and usefulness assessed against the topics families actually ask about
Findings recorded with concrete plain-language fixes; no PII collected and no engagement/tracking metrics
Material changes from the pilot trigger re-review per the version-scoped dual sign-off gate
